items.title: 'All you want as a parent': The disability program leading to home ownership

children.content: Not-for-profit Project Independence uses residents' disability support pension to share in a property, giving them legal ownership of their home and allowing them to build equity over time.

gallery.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

items.title: The skis being made from three types of Australian-grown timber

children.content: A couple in the NSW Snowy Mountains makes skis entirely from Australian-grown timber, though manufacturing for the snow industry in a "summer country" comes with its share of challenges.

image.altText: A man and a woman holding skis in a workshop setting, with a small baby.

items.title: New scooters and e-bikes have arrived in Canberra. Here's what that means

children.content: With Neuron's orange scooters having left Canberra, Lime has introduced a fleet of green scooters and e-bikes across the capital in partnership with the ACT government.

image.altText: A person rides a green Lime bike near a fountain in an urban space.

items.title: 'Privilege of my life': ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury quitting politics

children.content: The leader of the ACT Greens and former attorney-general Shane Rattenbury has stepped down from his position and says he is quitting politics.

gallery.altText: A man in a charcoal suit and green tie stands behind two microphone stands speaking seriously.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: Arrest warrant issued in legal battle for $27k in unpaid long service leave

children.content: It's now been over three years since two women left their jobs at a small business. Two courts later, they want their combined $27,000 in long service leave paid out to them, and say they won't give up until they get it.

image.altText: two women sitting at a dining table looking through paperwork

items.title: Two men found guilty of manslaughter over 1999 death of Irma Palasics

children.content: Two Melbourne men are found guilty of the manslaughter of Canberra woman Irma Palasics during a violent home invasion in 1999.

image.altText: Irma Palasics smiles at the camera, she has short curly hair and wears a pink knit vest.

items.title: 'Feel it when I breathe': Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program opens

children.content: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

items.title: 'Pretty invisible': The rise of ADHD medication being prescribed to young women

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

image.altText: A graphic image with silhouettes of men and women's side profiles overlaid with medication and two upwards arrows.

items.title: NRL overhauls training rules in landmark player safety move

children.content: The move to restrict training loads is aimed at mitigating injuries, including player head injuries, which continue to be a major issue for all contact sports.

image.altText: a rugby league coach at training

items.title: US singer D4vd charged with murdering 14yo girl found in his Tesla

children.content: The 21-year-old, who rose to fame after going viral on TikTok, is facing charges including first-degree murder, lewd and lascivious acts with an individual under 14, and mutilating a body.

image.altText: A man sits on a couch wearing a dark bandana, vlack shorts, a yellow mesh top and sunglasses.

items.title: Gas tax fight escalates ahead of budget as inquiry begins

children.content: Calls are growing for a new 25 per cent tax on gas exports to increase the petroleum resource rent tax on windfall profits, but companies have warned any changes would jeopardise Australia's energy supply.

items.title: For the first time in decades, cash use in Australia has increased

children.content: A new report has found cash use has dropped by more than 50 per cent in almost 20 years. But there has been a slight increase in cash payments since 2022.

image.altText: Australian dollar notes.

items.title: Japan warns of increased risk of mega-quake after 7.7-magnitude one

items.title: Fair Work Commission makes 'historic' fuel order for truck drivers
